title: Benchmark updates its best ideas list
content:
[Close Up Image Of A Stock Market Graph]
solarseven/iStock via Getty Images

Benchmark Equity Research on Tuesday released its quarterly update to the firm’s “best ideas” list, featuring 29 high-conviction, catalyst-driven stock picks selected by the company’s analysts.

The April 2026 refresh includes three notable additions to the list: IMAX Corp. (IMAX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IMAX]), CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWD]), and Lifeway Foods (LWAY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LWAY]). Vital Farms (VITL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VITL]) was removed from the list.

title: Strong Analyst Sentiment on indie Semiconductor (INDI) Amid $150 Million Convertible Senior Notes Pricing Announcement
content:
indie Semiconductor, Inc. (NASDAQ:INDI) is included in our list of the 11 most oversold semiconductor stocks to buy now.Strong Analyst Sentiment on indie Semiconductor (INDI) Amid $150 Million Convertible Senior Notes Pricing Announcement

Copyright: zenstock / 123RF Stock Photo

Positive analyst sentiment continues to surround indie Semiconductor, Inc. (NASDAQ:INDI)’s stock, as 86% of analysts maintain bullish ratings on the stock as of March 13, 2026. The consensus price target of $6.00 implies 141.94% upside potential.

On March 4, 2026, indie Semiconductor, Inc. (NASDAQ:INDI) announced the pricing of $150 million of 4.00% convertible senior notes due 2031 via a private offering to qualified institutional buyers.

indie Semiconductor, Inc. (NASDAQ:INDI) granted initial purchasers a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $25.0 million in notes, with net proceeds estimated at approximately $145.1 million, rising to $169.4 million, in the event that the option is fully exercised.

It plans to use $107.8 million of the proceeds to repurchase $104 million of principal of its 4.50% convertible senior notes due 2027. Meanwhile, the remaining proceeds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes, including potential acquisitions.

indie Semiconductor, Inc. (NASDAQ:INDI) develops automotive and software solutions for ADAS, driver automation, electrification, and in-cabin user experience, while expanding its portfolio of edge sensors, consisting of lidar, radar, ultrasound, and computer vision.

While we acknowledge the potential of INDI as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock. title: Is Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
content:
Is LRCX a good stock to buy? We came across a bullish thesis on Lam Research Corporation on Nikhs’s Substack. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on LRCX. Lam Research Corporation's share was trading at $199.33 as of March 6th. LRCX’s trailing and forward P/E were 40.93 and 29.41, respectively according to Yahoo Finance.Is Indie Semiconductor, Inc. (INDI) One of the Best High Return Penny Stocks to Buy Right Now?

Lam Research Corporation designs, manufactures, markets, refurbishes, and services semiconductor processing equipment used in the fabrication of integrated circuits in the United States and internationally. LRCX is emerging as a key beneficiary of the AI-driven semiconductor infrastructure buildout, presenting a compelling bullish case.

The company reported $5.34 billion in revenue, 49.7% gross margins, and $1.27 EPS for Q4 2025, all above expectations, and guided Q3 to $5.7 billion with margins holding at 49%, signaling durable growth. LRCX benefits from ten consecutive quarters of revenue growth, a record installed base exceeding 100,000 chambers, and strong process intensity per wafer that compounds with each node, independent of wafer demand.

Read More: 15 AI Stocks That Are Quietly Making Investors RichRead More:Undervalued AI Stock Poised For Massive Gains

This positions Lam as a “toll booth” on the multi-year AI infrastructure expansion, capturing structural growth rather than cyclical fluctuations. Key customers like SK Hynix confirm supply constraints, with memory production unable to meet demand, accelerating upgrades and expansion projects that require Lam’s etch and deposition tools, particularly for advanced DRAM and NAND nodes. The company is strategically shifting away from mature China revenue toward high-value advanced-node business, which, although slightly lower margin, strengthens long-term positioning in cutting-edge AI infrastructure.

NAND demand is also accelerating as storage evolves into AI compute memory, creating a multi-billion-dollar opportunity beyond traditional logic and DRAM segments. At $252, LRCX trades at a P/E that reflects only partial recognition of these structural tailwinds.

With earnings growth driven by both wafer intensity and AI infrastructure expansion, Lam is poised for a structural repricing, offering upside to $300–350 if NAND greenfield expansion materializes and the AI cycle continues to accelerate. The combination of durable demand, process-driven moat, and accelerating AI adoption makes Lam Research a compelling growth-oriented investment with significant risk/reward skew.

Story Continues

Previously, we covered a bullish thesis on Lam Research Corporation (LRCX) by The Antifragile Investor in May 2025, highlighting its dominance in semiconductor equipment, high-margin service flywheel, and embedded tools across major fabs. LRCX’s stock has appreciated by approximately 140.76% since then. Nikhs shares a similar view but emphasizes AI-driven structural growth, wafer process intensity, and multi-year expansion opportunities that could reprice the stock.

Lam Research Corporation is not on our list of the 40 Most Popular Stocks Among Hedge Funds. As per our database, 104 hedge fund portfolios held LRCX at the end of the fourth quarter which was 93 in the previous quarter.While we acknowledge the potential of LRCX as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

Disclosure: None.

title: Is ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) A Good Stock To Buy Now?
content:
Is ASML a good stock to buy? We came across a bullish thesis on ASML Holding N.V. on Vasileios Prassas’s Substack by Vasileios. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on ASML. ASML Holding N.V.'s share was trading at $1,357.42 as of March 9th. ASML’s trailing and forward P/E were 44.97 and 37.74, respectively according to Yahoo Finance.Is indie Semiconductor (INDI) One of the Most Popular AI Penny Stocks to Buy

ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) is the world’s most critical supplier of lithography machines used in semiconductor manufacturing, making it a foundational player in the global digital economy. Chips begin as silicon wafers and are built layer by layer through repeated manufacturing steps, with lithography acting as the “pen” that prints microscopic patterns onto each layer.

ASML dominates this step, holding a near-monopoly in advanced lithography and being the sole provider of extreme ultraviolet (EUV) machines, which use 13.5nm wavelength light to produce the smallest features required for modern chips.

These machines are essential for leading semiconductor manufacturers such as TSMC, Intel, Samsung, and SK Hynix, and the company continues pushing the technological frontier with its next-generation High NA EUV systems that further improve precision and transistor density. ASML generates roughly 75% of its revenue from machine sales and 25% from servicing them, creating a hybrid model of transactional and recurring revenue tied to long-term semiconductor fabrication planning cycles.

Over the last twelve months through the third quarter of 2025, ASML reported approximately €32 billion in revenue, gross margins of about 51–52%, and net income of €9.2 billion, reflecting strong profitability supported by an unparalleled technological moat. That moat is reinforced by extreme engineering complexity, deep customer integration, massive capital and time barriers to entry, and a highly specialized supplier ecosystem developed over decades.

The bullish thesis centers on structural growth drivers that are expected to expand lithography demand over the next decade, including the rise of AI computing, the migration of memory chips to EUV, global government investment in semiconductor manufacturing capacity, and increasing chip complexity that requires more layers and more lithography passes per wafer. As chips become larger, more sophisticated, and increasingly stacked in three-dimensional architectures, semiconductor manufacturers will need more lithography capacity, while ASML simultaneously benefits from rising machine prices as the industry transitions from DUV to EUV and High NA systems.

Story Continues

This combination of higher machine demand, greater pricing power, and expanding margins positions ASML to grow faster than the broader semiconductor industry over the long term. While risks such as semiconductor cyclicality, geopolitical export restrictions, and concentrated customers remain, ASML’s technological leadership and irreplaceable role in chip manufacturing make it one of the most strategically important companies in the global technology supply chain making it an attractive stock for long term investors.

Previously, we covered a bullish thesis on ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) by Monopolistic Investor in May 2025, which highlighted the company’s leadership in EUV lithography machines, its unmatched engineering complexity, strong pricing power, and strategic importance in the semiconductor supply chain. ASML’s stock price has appreciated by approximately 81.83% since our coverage. Vasileios shares a similar view but emphasizes on long-term growth driven by AI demand, chip complexity, and High NA EUV adoption.

ASML Holding N.V. is not on our list of the 40 Most Popular Stocks Among Hedge Funds. As per our database, 101 hedge fund portfolios held ASML at the end of the fourth quarter which was 82 in the previous quarter. While we acknowledge the risk and potential of ASML as an investment, our conviction lies in the belief that some AI stocks hold greater promise for delivering higher returns and doing so within a shorter time frame. If you are looking for an AI stock that is more promising than ASML and that has 10,000% upside potential, check out our report about this cheapest AI stock.
